rulings.Case Title: KEITH PULLIAM VS. FRANCISCO MEXICAN FOODS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Judgment Creditor Keith Pulliams Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Assigning To His Attorneys, Law Offices Of Mary Catherine Wiederhold, The May 22, 2024 Judgment, An Order Assigning The Rents
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF KEITH PULLIAM Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Assigning To His Attorneys, Law Offices Of Mary Catherine Wiederhold, The May 22, 2024 Judgment, An Order Assigning The Rents is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a Court order is required or is proper for an assignment under CCP 954. Moving party fails to provide admissible evidence that the judgment debtor has a right to payment of rents generated by the three properties mentioned in the application. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MM150016TH LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. TALMADGE AND TALMADGE, INC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2025 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT GINA GIAMMANCO (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS "GINA GIAMMANCO, AN INDIVIDUAL DOING BUSINESS AS URBAN HOLISTIC"), KRISTIN JAMIESON MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith viable claims for equitable indemnity, contribution and declaratory relief if they may do so in accordance with the applicable law including Stop Loss Ins. Broker v. Brown & Toland Med. Group (2066) 143 Cal.App.4th 1036, CCP 875 and CCP 1060. Moving party is ordered to comply with CCP 439(a)(3) in the future. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
